frameworks.name: mixture-of-agents reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: model-free and model-based agents combined as mixture generating behavior

frameworks.name: hidden Markov model
frameworks.one_line: latent states capture temporal shifts in agent mixture weights

frameworks.name: model-based vs model-free RL
frameworks.one_line: dual-process framing of automaticity versus deliberation

core_question: How do contributions of model-free and model-based agents shift dynamically within a reward-learning session?
key_assumptions: behavior arises from a mixture of competing RL agents
agent contributions can change across discrete hidden states
action values are inferred jointly with state dynamics
manipulations: within-session time as a latent-state variable
key_findings: rats progress from initial model-based exploration to model-based exploitation
later states show reduced task engagement
inferred hidden states predict changes in response time
inferred states predict shifts in OFC neural encoding
interpretations: static mixture-of-agents models miss within-session strategy shifts
MoA-HMM links latent strategy states to behavior and OFC activity
